intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Buxella brachycera. However, I can provide information on a related plant, Buxus sempervirens (common boxwood). 

Buxus sempervirens is a slow-growing, evergreen shrub or small tree native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Buxus sempervirens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Buxus sempervirens is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and topicals, as well as being used in traditional medicine and as a component in some aromatherapy products.
